title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Boston duck boat overturned in Cambridge on a boat ramp, injuring 11 people, one seriously, at 10 Museum Way.
The duck boat was being towed after a breakdown when the tow rope snapped, causing it to tip over.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the day and is reviewing the incident with authorities.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins traded two first-round picks to acquire winger JJ Peterka from Utah.
Peterka has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three years in the NHL.
Boston sees Peterka as a valuable addition, adding speed and scoring potential to their roster.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering GLP-1 and anti-obesity drugs for weight loss starting July 1, affecting 22,000 members.
The policy change, part of Gov. Healey's fiscal 2027 budget, aims to save $15 million annually.
Massachusetts Medicaid was one of 14 state programs covering anti-obesity drugs without other conditions, trend now reversing.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins selected seven prospects in Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft, including two goalies and several wingers.
Second-round pick Yuri Ivanov, a goalie from MHL, will stay in Russia before joining the Bruins in future years.
Bruins' seventh-round pick Cullen McCrate, a defenseman from USHL, will play for Michigan State in the 2026-27 season.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela resulted in at least 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing, per Associated Press reports.
Red Sox players, including Venezuelan natives, showed support by writing messages on their hats during recent games.
David Ortiz raised funds at Fenway Park for Venezuelan earthquake relief, urging fans to contribute through the Red Sox Foundation.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized analytics on X, saying they are used to discredit him and others in the NBA.
Bobby Marks shared an analytics report stating Jaylen Brown would be the seventh-best player on an NBA team.
Brown, a top-10 MVP candidate last season, is a potential trade target but remains key to Boston's competitiveness.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Yankees 4-1, winning their third straight game against the AL leaders.
Jake Bennett pitched 6.1 innings for the Red Sox, allowing three hits and one run to earn his second career win.
Red Sox hitters Masataka Yoshida and Anthony Seigler both homered, while Willson Contreras hit a two-run double.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded forward Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Lysell, a 2021 first-round pick, played 12 NHL games for Boston, recording one goal and two assists.
Ivan, 23, had 26 points in 66 AHL games last season and aims to bring versatility to the Bruins.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium, showcasing their attacking prowess.
Kylian Mbappe provided key assists, while Ousmane Dembélé added goals, solidifying France's status as tournament favorites.
Despite Norway's higher xG, France's efficient attack and depth contributed to their commanding victory.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich police and the Cape & Islands District Attorney's Office are investigating threats made by a student at Sandwich Middle High.
Authorities took action after GoGuardian flagged threatening language on a school-issued device, ensuring student and staff safety.
The student involved has been removed from school, and the families of targeted students have been informed and are cooperating.
